value.found_quote:
Cross-Platform Command & Path Matching
Antigravity ensures your permission rules work flawlessly whether you are developing on macOS, Linux, or Windows. On macOS and Linux, paths use standard forward slashes (`/`). On Windows, Antigravity automatically normalizes paths prior to rule evaluation by stripping drive letters (e.g., `C:`) and converting all backslashes (`\`) to forward slashes (`/`).
value.rationale: The page (Google Antigravity docs) describes a "Cross-Platform Command & Path Matching" feature that works across macOS, Linux, and Windows, including normalization of Windows paths (stripping drive letters, converting backslashes to forward slashes). This substantiates the claim of cross-platform command and path normalization. It is presented as a current capability with no deprecation or preview qualifier.
